title: Coelogyne graminea
taxonomy: Coelogyne graminea is classified within the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. This family includes approximately 30,000 species of orchids, which are divided into about 763 genera. Orchids are further grouped into several subfamilies, with Epidendroideae being one of the largest subfamilies. Within the Orchidaceae family, Coelogyne graminea is placed in the subtribe Coelogyneinae. The species is part of the genus Coelogyne, which contains about 200 species of orchids.
uses: Coelogyne graminea is known for its potential medicinal uses, particularly in traditional Asian medicine. It is believed to possess antibacterial and antifungal properties, which may help in treating various infections. Additionally, it is thought to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it a potential remedy for various health conditions. The plant is also used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various ailments, including fever and skin conditions. Further research is needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of Coelogyne graminea for medicinal purposes.

intro: Coelogyne graminea, commonly known as the grass-petaled Coelogyne, is a species of orchid native to the Himalayas and Southeast Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine, where it is believed to have anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and antimicrobial effects, potentially aiding in the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. Coelogyne graminea contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, with the most common preparations being a tea or a decoction of the dried roots and stems.
